title: Build cross-compilation fixes
body:
Fixes #16702 

Fixes included:
* Build documentation for the same target OS-ARCH as the requested application binaries (needed tools are still built for host)
* Limit msi build to amd64 arch as msitools doesn't support arm64 Windows as of now
* Fixes for HOME and PATH variables having whitespaces inside.

Now these commands work on MacOS M1:
* `make clean podman.msi`
* `make clean podman-remote-release-windows_arm64.zip`
* `make clean podman-remote-release-windows_amd64.zip`

Signed-off-by: Arthur Sengileyev <arthur.sengileyev@gmail.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
Will look into

```
ln -sf /private/var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/docs/source/markdown/links docs/build/man/
docs/remote-docs.sh \
		darwin \
		docs/build/remote/darwin \
		docs/build/man
docs/remote-docs.sh: line 106: bin/darwin/podman: Bad CPU type in executable
docs: no doc file nor link docs/build/man/podman-.1 for 'podman '
```

Removed explicit GOARCH passed in CI as it now should correctly be auto detected inside build step and this only affected the arch of utilities built for execution on build host.

I manually checked architecture of podman binary inside archives on my local machine.
Probably in some places I got rosetta2 used for tools locally. Will check in more details.
Will need to check it on a device w/o rosetta or on Intel Mac with cross to arm.

> Will need to check it on a device w/o rosetta or on Intel Mac with cross to arm.

Other than not building/using the "release" artifacts, all of CI does use the same build machinery, so the artifacts are highly-likely okay.

oh actually.... @ashley-cui you have an intel-mac don't you?  Would you mind taking a quick peek at the mac build here and make sure it runs?
(once they build)

> docs/remote-docs.sh: line 106: bin/darwin/podman: Bad CPU type in executable

This is likely the GOARCH being wrong, just based on the error message.

> This is likely the GOARCH being wrong, just based on the error message.

It was another funny thing. Here doc steps immediately builds podman-remote with settings applied https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/Makefile#L481 Which is fine to verify that we can compile, etc. 

Next line tries to build it for host https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/Makefile#L482, but if the host is the same OS it will detect already built binary, which could come for different arch. We need to force remove binaries first and then compile again to prevent this collision.

It could be refactored to something better, but would lead to a lot more changes, I guess.

Pushed another fix. In `podman.msi` it was using single pass for binaries and docs, which can't work reliably due to described above way how documentation works (it needs to remove binaries, build native ones, generate docs and only then we need to build binaries (removing native versions, as they might conflict).

> but if the host is the same OS it will detect already built binary,

Oh right! I remember hitting this.  `make` is absolutely ignorant about the binary being "correct", it only cares that file exists and the timestamp is correct.  I think removing the file is fine (IIRC, we do that elsewhere already).

> and only then we need to build binaries (removing native versions, as they might conflict).

Yep, same issue, glad you caught it.

I'm amazed you still have any hair-left.  It took me months to grow mine back :rofl:
All cross-builds seems to pass. I can squash commits and force push, because feeling a bit guilty for having 3 commits for not so many changes 😅
Yes, please squash.  Some don't, but this gang prefers it that way.  Thanks.

Tested the binary on x86 and looks like it works, and i tested building on x86 and it works. 
Though, it looks like the target generates some empty folders inside the .zip file, but I think that's an existing bug. 

LGTM, Thanks @arixmkii!
